one_time_description: Loan insurance proceeds used to repay RBC loans financing Treaty 6 Agricultural Benefits Claim and Flood Claim
economic_notes: Willow Cree Developments Limited Partnership (100% owned) generated net income of $314,147 on revenue of $5,654,635. Capital projects underway include Water Treatment Plant Upgrades, Veterans Hall, Tipi Lodges, WIFI Project, Sewage Lagoon, and CMHC Phases 12 and 13. Subsequent to year-end, Chief and Headman Salaries claim settled with Canada for $4,101,046.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2022. Nation is in Saskatchewan (Treaty Six Territory), not Alberta. Consolidated entity includes Willow Cree Health Services Corporation, CMHC Housing Program, and Legacy Trust. Willow Cree Developments LP accounted for by modified equity method (GBE). Loan insurance proceeds of $2,912,752 recorded as other income (one-time item) used to repay RBC loans. Emphasis of Matter in auditor report regarding dispute with former professional services provider over ~$1,200,000 in claimed fees related to Specific Claims litigation. Subsequent event: Chief and Headman Salaries claim settled for $4,101,046 after year-end. 38 contaminated sites identified but no liability recorded. total_government_transfers estimated as sum of ISC + FNIHB + CMHC + Innovation Canada + Prairie Spirit + Correction Services + Government of Saskatchewan + Other government agencies + FSIN + Public Health Agency of Canada + College of Physicians + Ministry of Justice + Dept of Justice RCMP + Dept of Justice Canada + Treaty Six Education Council.
